senses.definition: Very old; from a time long ago (archaic spelling of ancient).
examples: The town was full of antient buildings and narrow streets.
In the poem, an antient king rules a faraway land.

senses.definition: A very old person (archaic spelling of ancient).
examples: The young soldiers listened carefully to the tales of the antient.
An antient sat by the fire, telling stories of war.

senses.definition: Strong dislike or opposition to religious enthusiasm or emotional religious behavior, especially in historical discussions.
examples: Writers of the period often expressed antienthusiasm toward radical religious movements.
The sermon reflected a clear antienthusiasm for emotional preaching.

senses.definition: A person who strongly dislikes or opposes religious enthusiasm or intense emotional belief.
examples: As an antienthusiast, he distrusted dramatic conversions and ecstatic visions.
Many antienthusiasts argued for a more rational form of religion.

senses.definition: Showing strong distrust of or opposition to religious enthusiasm or intense emotional belief.
examples: The movement provoked an antienthusiastic response from church leaders.
His antienthusiastic tone criticized emotional preaching.

senses.definition: In a way that shows strong distrust of or opposition to religious enthusiasm or emotional belief.
examples: The pamphlet antienthusiastically attacked revival meetings.
He wrote antienthusiastically about visionary experiences.

senses.definition: Opposing or reducing entropy, especially in scientific or mathematical discussions.
examples: The model describes an antientropic process that increases order.
Some physicists have proposed antientropic forces in the early universe.

senses.definition: Plural of antient; very old people or things (archaic spelling of ancients).
examples: The poem speaks of the antients who first settled the land.
Legends of the antients were passed down through generations.

senses.definition: Strong opposition to environmentalism or to policies intended to protect the environment.
examples: The party's antienvironmentalism was clear in its support for heavy industry.
Antienvironmentalism often appears in debates over climate policy.

senses.definition: A person who is strongly opposed to environmentalism or environmental protection policies.
examples: Antienvironmentalists criticized regulations on coal mining.
He is known as an antienvironmentalist in local politics.

senses.definition: Plural of antienvironmentalist; people who strongly oppose environmentalism or green policies.
examples: Antienvironmentalists argued that the new rules would cost jobs.
Some antienvironmentalists question the science of climate change.

senses.definition: Able to block or reduce the action of enzymes.
examples: The drug has antienzymatic properties that slow digestion.
Researchers tested several antienzymatic compounds in the lab.

senses.definition: A substance that blocks or reduces the activity of an enzyme.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antienzyme to reduce inflammation.
Antienzymes can help control certain metabolic processes.

senses.definition: Having the quality of blocking or reducing enzyme activity.
examples: They studied the antienzymic effect of the new compound.
Antienzymic agents are used in some medical treatments.

senses.definition: A point on the Earth's surface opposite the epicenter of an earthquake, on the far side of the planet.
examples: Seismologists calculated the antiepicenter of the large earthquake.
The antiepicenter lies in the southern hemisphere, far from any land.

senses.definition: The prevention or treatment of epilepsy.
examples: New antiepilepsy research focuses on safer medications.
She is taking part in an antiepilepsy drug trial.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or control epileptic seizures.
examples: She takes antiepileptic medicine every day.
Doctors adjusted his antiepileptic dose after the tests.

senses.definition: A drug used to prevent or control epileptic seizures.
examples: This antiepileptic works well for many patients.
The doctor prescribed a new antiepileptic for him.

senses.definition: Drugs used to prevent or control epileptic seizures.
examples: Modern antiepileptics often have fewer side effects.
Doctors may combine different antiepileptics to control seizures.

senses.definition: Opposed to bishops or to a church system led by bishops.
examples: The group published antiepiscopal pamphlets in the seventeenth century.
Her antiepiscopal views put her at odds with church leaders.

senses.definition: A person who opposes bishops or an episcopal church system.
examples: Many antiepiscopists supported a more democratic church structure.
The pamphlet was written by a well‑known antiepiscopist.

senses.definition: Damaging to epithelial tissue or acting against epithelial cells.
examples: The chemical showed antiepithelial effects in the lab.
Some treatments can have unwanted antiepithelial activity.

senses.definition: Designed to prevent or reduce erosion.
examples: The government funded antierosion projects along the coast.
Antierosion measures were put in place to protect the river banks.

senses.definition: Preventing or slowing down erosion.
examples: Antierosive coatings protect the metal from damage.
Farmers use antierosive methods to keep soil in place.

senses.definition: Deliberately avoiding or opposed to sexual excitement or erotic content.
examples: The director chose an antierotic style for the film.
Some critics described the novel as strangely antierotic.

senses.definition: Used to prevent or treat erysipelas, a bacterial skin infection.
examples: Antierysipelas serum was once widely used in hospitals.
Doctors tested an antierysipelas vaccine on farm animals.

senses.definition: Opposed to the traditional power structure or ruling groups in society.
examples: The band was known for its antiestablishment lyrics.
Many young voters support antiestablishment candidates.

senses.definition: A drug that blocks the effects of estrogen in the body.
examples: The doctor prescribed an antiestrogen after the surgery.
Antiestrogens are often used in breast cancer treatment.

senses.definition: Drugs that block the effects of estrogen in the body.
examples: Researchers are testing new antiestrogens for breast cancer.
Some antiestrogens can cause hot flashes as a side effect.

senses.definition: The site of a major American Civil War battle in Maryland in 1862.
examples: Antietam is often called the bloodiest single day in American military history.
We visited Antietam National Battlefield on our trip.

senses.definition: Showing strong dislike of or prejudice against a particular ethnic group.
examples: The law was criticized as antiethnic and discriminatory.
Antiethnic violence broke out in the region.

senses.definition: Opposed to eugenics or to attempts to control human reproduction for genetic reasons.
examples: Antieugenic arguments stressed human rights and dignity.
The policy reflects a strong antieugenic stance.

senses.definition: Strongly critical of or opposed to evangelical Christians or evangelical beliefs.
examples: The article took an openly antievangelical tone.
Some antievangelical groups campaign for stricter church‑state separation.

senses.definition: Opposition to the theory of evolution, especially in education or religion.
examples: Antievolution campaigns have challenged science curricula.
The book traces the history of antievolution movements.

senses.definition: Related to or showing opposition to evolutionary theory.
examples: He gave an antievolutional lecture at the conference.
Antievolutional views remain strong in some regions.

senses.definition: In a way that shows opposition to evolutionary theory.
examples: The textbook was written antievolutionally, ignoring modern biology.
He spoke antievolutionally about human origins.

senses.definition: Opposed to or rejecting the theory of evolution.
examples: Antievolutionary beliefs shape the school's science policy.
She wrote an antievolutionary article for the magazine.

senses.definition: A set of beliefs or a movement that rejects evolutionary theory.
examples: The book analyzes antievolutionism in the twentieth century.
Antievolutionism often appears in debates over school curricula.

senses.definition: Different forms or versions of opposition to evolutionary theory.
examples: Scholars distinguish several antievolutionisms within modern religious thought.
The article compares American and European antievolutionisms.

senses.definition: A person who rejects or strongly opposes evolutionary theory.
examples: Antievolutionists campaign to change biology textbooks.
The debate featured an antievolutionist and a biologist.

senses.definition: Describing ideas or actions that are strongly opposed to evolution.
examples: The law had clear antievolutionistic roots.
Her antievolutionistic writing influenced many readers.

senses.definition: People who reject or strongly oppose evolutionary theory.
examples: Antievolutionists organized protests at the school board.
Some antievolutionists promote alternative creation stories.

senses.definition: Opposition to expansion, often in business, politics, or territory.
examples: Antiexpansion sentiment grew after the financial crisis.
The report documents antiexpansion movements in the region.

senses.definition: A policy or belief that a country or organization should not expand its power or territory.
examples: The party's antiexpansionism shaped its foreign policy.
Antiexpansionism became popular after costly wars.

senses.definition: A person who opposes the expansion of a country or organization.
examples: Antiexpansionists warned against overseas adventures.
She is an outspoken antiexpansionist in parliament.

senses.definition: Opposed to expansion of a country or organization.
examples: The government adopted an antiexpansionist policy.
Antiexpansionist views influenced the treaty.

senses.definition: Opposing or restricting the export of goods.
examples: Antiexporting policies were introduced to protect local consumers.
The government took an antiexporting stance on medical supplies.

senses.definition: Opposition to expressionism, especially as an art or literary movement.
examples: Antiexpressionism emerged as critics turned away from extreme emotion in art.
The essay explores antiexpressionism in early twentieth‑century painting.

senses.definition: Opposed to expressionism as an artistic or literary style.
examples: Antiexpressionist critics called for a return to realism.
Her work belongs to an antiexpressionist tradition.

senses.definition: A person who opposes expressionism or prefers other artistic styles.
examples: Antiexpressionists argued that the style was too emotional.
The panel included both expressionists and antiexpressionists.

senses.definition: Showing or based on opposition to expressionistic styles in art or literature.
examples: The director took an antiexpressionistic approach, avoiding dramatic gestures.
Antiexpressionistic trends favored calm, simple images.

senses.definition: Deliberately not emotional or not showing feelings or ideas clearly.
examples: The painting has a cold, antiexpressive quality.
His antiexpressive face hid any sign of joy or anger.

senses.definition: In a way that avoids showing feelings or clear expression.
examples: She spoke antiexpressively, with almost no change in tone.
The actor moved antiexpressively, giving the scene a strange calm.

senses.definition: The quality of not showing feelings or ideas clearly, especially as a style or deliberate choice.
examples: The film's antiexpressiveness makes the violence seem even colder.
Critics praised the novel's controlled antiexpressiveness.
